key_insight: Strain-space hyperreduction can extend efficient reduced-order simulation to large-deformation solid mechanics, delivering much better runtime-accuracy tradeoffs than displacement-space sampling methods.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, accurate nonlinear solid mechanics simulations with parameterized loads and material behavior, which is useful for design, optimization, and digital-twin workflows.
method: Use boundary-consistent lifting fields offline to satisfy arbitrary parameterized Dirichlet conditions by construction.
Form ECM, E3C, and EMSL directly in strain space rather than displacement space.
Compare strain-space hyperreduction against standard ECSW on large-deformation hyperelastic test problems.
evidence_signal: On two hyperelastic benchmark problems with parameterized material behavior and deformation, the strain-space methods outperformed displacement-space ECSW; EMSL achieved about 100,000x speedup and E3C about 10,000x while maintaining high accuracy.
watch_item: The approach relies on offline-computed lifting fields and reduced strain-space sampling, so setup cost and robustness of the offline stage may be important in practice.
paper_title: Efficient strain-space hyperreduction in large-deformation solid mechanics
